text:
any behavioural issues noted or observed or welfare concerns regarding the child[ren] (exhibiting any contemporaneous records);

any other matters relevant to the child[ren]’s welfare.

Section 91(14) Order

Pursuant to section 91(14) of the Children Act 1989 no application in respect of the child[ren] for [a child arrangements order] / [a specific issue order] / [a prohibited steps order] / [permission to cause the child to be known by a new surname] / [permission to remove the child from the United Kingdom] / [insert other type of order] / [an order under any [other] provision of the Children Act 1989] / [, [and] variation or discharge of those orders] / [, and enforcement of those orders] shall be made by [name] without the court’s permission until [time and date] / [[the youngest] child attains the age of 16] / [further order].

Any application for permission to apply shall, in the first instance, not be served on the respondent[s] and shall be considered on the papers. If the court is satisfied that the application shows sufficient merit, the application for permission to apply shall be served on the respondents and listed for an on notice hearing.

Any application for permission to apply is reserved to [name of judge] if available.

(insert as a recital)

The reasons for making the s.91(14) order are [as set out in the judgment handed down today.] / [as set out in the judgment given today, a transcript of which have been ordered.] / [as follows:

order [insert reasons]]

Separate orders

Separate orders have been made today for:

Disclosure of the address of [name] by 4.00pm on [date].

Disclosure of the parents’ medical records by 4.00pm on [date].

Disclosure of the child[ren]’s medical records by 4.00pm on [date].

A report to be provided by [name of agency] by 4.00pm on [date].

Police disclosure by 4.00pm on [date].

Settlement conference
